title: podman build --output type=local,dest=/some/dir doesn't work with remote podman
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman build --output type=local,dest=/some/dir doesn't work with remote podman. One might expect that this feature is not dependant on whether we use a local podman or a remote podman, as both `podman build`, `podman create` and `podman cp` work fine with remote podman, which are the basic operation required to implement the behavior of `podman build --output type=local,dest=/some/dir`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

(tested with the `quay.io/podman/upstream` image)

1. Setup a dummy build context:

```
[root@56ec07344378 /]# mkdir src
[root@56ec07344378 /]# cat >/src/Dockerfile <<EOF
> FROM fedora as base
> RUN echo "some value" > /some-content
> FROM scratch
> COPY --from=base /some-content /some-content
> EOF
[root@56ec07344378 /]# podman system service -t 0 &
[1] 21
```

2. Try in non remote mode, it works fine

```
[root@56ec07344378 /]# mkdir output
[root@56ec07344378 /]# podman build --output type=local,dest=output src
[1/2] STEP 1/2: FROM fedora AS base
Resolved "fedora" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 62946078034b done
Copying config 2ecb6df959 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[1/2] STEP 2/2: RUN echo "some value" > /some-content
--> aa2dd23d68f
[2/2] STEP 1/2: FROM scratch
[2/2] STEP 2/2: COPY --from=base /some-content /some-content
[2/2] COMMIT
--> b0675c7a98b
b0675c7a98b0ca93a7914929e2d3ef395363d83ef3b738199a585640fe274edb
[root@56ec07344378 /]# cat output/some-content
some value
```

3. Try in remote mode, it's refused in the early command line parsing logic:

```
[root@56ec07344378 /]# podman --remote build --output type=local,dest=output src
Error: '--output' option is not supported in remote mode
```

**Describe the results you received:**

`podman build --output type=local,dest=/some/dir` doesn't work with remote podman.

**Describe the results you expected:**

`podman build --output type=local,dest=/some/dir` should work even with remote podman.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
[root@56ec07344378 /]# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0-dev
API Version:  4.3.0-dev
Go Version:   go1.18.6
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
Hi @Romain-Geissler-1A , One issue is that usually the `remote` machine is a VM or a server running away from host, the option `type=local` would not make much sense it will generate the artifact on the `remote` machine end and not the client which created the request.

Could you let me know what was your expectation here ? Were you trying to generate artifact on the remote side itself ?

Hi,

No for me the logical thing would be the other way around. What matters is the machine where the client runs, where the user "thinks" he is, so "local" refers to the client machine. This behavior is what buildx implements when using a "remote" docker. So technically it's a "podman build" followed by a logic similar to the one of "podman cp", to fetch back files on the machine where the client runs.

Cheers,
Romain
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

For anyone else trying to get this to work on Mac or Windows, the [docs](https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/markdown/podman-build.1.html) state:

>(This option is not available with the remote Podman client, including Mac and Windows (excluding WSL2) machines)
https://github.com/abiosoft/colima with docker cli have worked amazingly for me.
